evidence.scientific_clarity: All three numbered equations (ELBO, decomposition, retrieval-augmented variant) are rendered with correct LaTeX math typesetting; derivation chain is clear and readable.
evidence.typography: pdfTeX produces native Type 1 Latin Modern fonts (LMRoman, LMMathItalic, MSBM) — all embedded, subsetted, unicode-mapped; no DejaVu Sans; superior to fonttype=42.
evidence.palette: Pure black-on-white mathematical document; no color needed; no directional/categorical color discipline issues.
evidence.layout_density: PDF is 2 pages (pdfinfo: Pages=2) despite brief requiring a single-page output; content overflows, suggesting layout was not constrained to one page.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption uses \eqref cross-references matching equation labels; describes each equation accurately; however 2-page PDF violates the brief's single-page requirement which Reviewer 2 would flag.
evidence.vector_fidelity: Genuine pdfTeX PDF 1.7 with 16 fully-embedded Type 1 font subsets; all fonts emb=yes, sub=yes, uni=yes; text is fully selectable and vector.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, caption.tex, gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial; equations.tex (the source TeX) is absent from the work directory.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains what each equation represents with specific mathematical descriptions (Jensen's inequality, Markov structure, k retrieved exemplars); lacks a quantitative/numerical finding since this is a derivation block, not a results figure.

overall_strengths: Genuine pdfTeX compilation produces real vector PDF with professional Latin Modern math fonts — all properly embedded and subsetted, far exceeding typical matplotlib output.
Caption accurately cross-references all three equations with \eqref labels and gives precise mathematical descriptions of each derivation step.
overall_weaknesses: PDF spans 2 pages instead of the required single page; the brief explicitly asks for a single-page derivation block and this overflows.
equations.tex source file is missing from the output directory, making the figure non-reproducible from the work directory alone.
